aliases: Beggar's Chicken with Wild Mushroom Stuffing

english: Beggar's Chicken with Wild Mushroom Stuffing

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Beggar's Chicken specifically; 'chicken dish' (FOODON_03315116) is the closest defensible ancestor class. Mushroom-stuffed clay-baked variant is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken, cooked — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for this clay-baked whole-chicken preparation; no specific code exists for Beggar's Chicken.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Beggar's Chicken or clay-baked stuffed chicken. A0CKP (cooked chicken) is used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this traditional Chinese dish preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant or banquet serving of Mushroom-Stuffed Beggar's Chicken is approximately 300 g, representing a generous portion of clay-baked chicken with mushroom stuffing, yielding roughly 495 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, whole, roasted, meat and skin (NDB No. 05013) combined with Mushrooms, shiitake, cooked (NDB No. 11268) as the closest approved-source composite analogue for this clay-baked stuffed chicken dish
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No approved source contains a direct entry for Mushroom-Stuffed Beggar's Chicken. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central using roasted whole chicken (meat and skin) as the primary base and cooked shiitake mushrooms (NDB No. 11268) as the stuffing component, weighted approximately 85:15 by mass. The input AI-generated values (energy 165 kcal, protein 18.5 g, fat 9.2 g, carbohydrate 2.5 g) are broadly consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA roasted chicken data (~2.6 g/100 g). Sodium 320 mg/100 g reflects soy-sauce marinade contribution and is plausible. All micronutrient values are carried from the input enriched JSON and cross-checked for plausibility against USDA chicken and mushroom entries.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on high-protein, low-carbohydrate cooked chicken dish with vegetable stuffing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mushroom-Stuffed Beggar's Chicken. GI is estimated at 25 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogues for high-protein, low-carbohydrate cooked poultry dishes. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with only 2.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g; the mushroom stuffing contributes dietary fibre which further attenuates glycaemic response. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (25 × 7.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.9 GL units (very low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce marinade (320 mg/100 g; ~960 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful that a 300 g serving contributes approximately 960 mg sodium — about 42% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — and should request reduced-soy-sauce preparation or limit portion size accordingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (2.5 g/100 g) and low glycemic index (~25) with high protein supporting satiety
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mushroom-Stuffed Beggar's Chicken is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low glycemic load (~1.9 per serving), high protein content (18.5 g/100 g), and dietary fibre from the mushroom stuffing, all of which support stable postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (9.2 g/100 g) including estimated saturated fat (~2.6 g/100 g) from chicken skin, partially offset by beneficial mushroom bioactives
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume this dish in moderation; removing the chicken skin reduces saturated fat intake, and the wild mushroom stuffing (shiitake, porcini) provides beta-glucans and antioxidants that may support cardiovascular health, but sodium from the marinade warrants attention.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory compounds and antioxidants from wild mushrooms
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Wild mushrooms such as shiitake and porcini provide ergothioneine and polyphenolic antioxidants that may modestly support anti-inflammatory pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mushroom-Stuffed Beggar's Chicken (菌菇叫化鸡 / 菌菇叫化雞, Jūn Gū Jiào Huā Jī) is a celebrated regional variation of the classic Jiangnan dish known as Beggar's Chicken (叫化鸡). The traditional preparation involves a whole chicken marinated in soy sauce, Shaoxing rice wine, ginger, and scallions, then stuffed — in this mushroom-centric version — with a luxurious mixture of rehydrated wild forest mushrooms including shiitake, wood ear, tea tree mushroom, and porcini. The stuffed chicken is wrapped tightly in lotus leaves to seal in moisture and aromatic compounds, then encased in a thick shell of clay or salt-dough before being slow-baked for several hours. The clay encasement creates a sealed steam environment that renders the chicken meat extraordinarily tender and infused with the deep, earthy umami of the mushroom stuffing. At the table, the clay shell is ceremonially cracked open, releasing fragrant steam — a theatrical presentation that makes this dish a centrepiece of celebratory banquets and festive family meals in Zhejiang and Jiangsu provinces. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (18.5 g/100 g), moderate in fat (9.2 g/100 g), and very low in carbohydrates (2.5 g/100 g), yielding approximately 165 kcal per 100 g. The mushroom stuffing contributes dietary fibre (1.2 g/100 g), B vitamins, selenium (22 mcg/100 g), and potassium (280 mg/100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 25 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content (320 mg/100 g) from the soy sauce marinade is the primary clinical consideration for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. A standard serving is approximately 300 g, providing around 495 kcal.

confidence_reason: No approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for Mushroom-Stuffed Beggar's Chicken. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central roasted whole chicken and cooked shiitake mushroom entries as the closest approved-source composite analogue. The input AI-generated values are broadly consistent with this composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central (roasted whole chicken, meat and skin; cooked shiitake mushrooms) as no direct approved-source entry exists for this dish. Input AI-generated values are consistent with this composite and retained. 2. Saturated fat estimated at 2.6 g/100 g from USDA roasted chicken data; monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid fractions and moisture data for this traditional clay-baked preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Beggar's Chicken exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. Sodium content (320 mg/100 g) reflects soy sauce marinade contribution and is the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. 5. The dish is not inherently halal-certified as Shaoxing rice wine (alcohol) is a traditional ingredient. 6. Duplicate portion entry in input JSON has been deduplicated to a single 300 g serving. 7. The input source 'Zhejiang University Culinary Science' (zju.edu.cn) is not on the approved source list and has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the nutrient evidence base.
